tasks.title: Define Narada-native to-data packet contract
tasks.goal: Define the shared read-only packet contract used by Narada-native to-data adapters.
tasks.context: The Narada-native carrier needs bounded, attributed data packets that can feed intelligence and orchestration without becoming mutation authority.
required_work: Specify shared packet envelope fields: schema, read_family, carrier_session_id, agent_id, source_surface, capability_ref, attribution, freshness, bounded_summary, raw_values_recorded=false, and authority_mutation_performed=false.
Define packet families for task_packet, work_next_peek, inbox_summary, readiness_snapshot, evidence_ref_summary, and bounded_file_excerpt.
Add schema fixtures and tests proving each family has attribution, capability posture, bounded evidence, and explicit no-mutation flags.
non_goals: Do not implement live Narada reads in this task.
Do not grant task, inbox, outbox, command, publication, repository, or credential mutation authority.
Do not record raw secrets, raw transcripts, or raw provider outputs.
acceptance_criteria: A shared to-data packet contract exists with explicit read family, attribution, capability, freshness, bounded summary, and no-mutation fields.
All required packet families are represented by fixtures or tests.
Tests prove raw_values_recorded is false and authority_mutation_performed is false for every packet family.

tasks.title: Implement task and work-next to-data readers
tasks.goal: Implement read-only task and work-next packet readers for Narada-native sessions.
tasks.context: The carrier must be able to inspect assigned work and possible next work without claiming, reporting, closing, or mutating lifecycle state.
required_work: Route task packet reads through narada task read --format json or an injected equivalent reader.
Route work selection through a no-claim peek surface when available, and return a refusal packet if only claim-capable work-next surfaces are available.
Record command/source attribution, cwd or Site root, requested task or agent, timestamp, and bounded field presence.
Add tests proving task claim, report, review, close, inbox, outbox, command, publication, and repository mutation flags remain false.
non_goals: Do not use direct SQLite reads as the normal task or work-next interface.
Do not record raw task markdown in carrier evidence.
Do not auto-claim work from the to-data reader.
acceptance_criteria: Task reads produce bounded to-data packets through canonical Narada surfaces.
Work-next reads refuse safely when no no-claim read surface is available.
Tests prove the readers do not mutate lifecycle, inbox, outbox, command, publication, or repository state.

tasks.title: Implement inbox summary to-data reader
tasks.goal: Implement a bounded read-only inbox summary reader for Narada-native sessions.
tasks.context: The carrier needs inbox awareness without gaining envelope transition authority or exposing raw payload values.
required_work: Read inbox state only through canonical inbox list, pending, read, or injected test readers, not direct SQLite as the normal path.
Summarize envelope ids, statuses, source refs, kind, target locus, and bounded summary fields.
Redact or omit raw secret-like values and unbounded payload text.
Add tests proving no inbox status transition occurs.
non_goals: Do not claim, triage, promote, archive, import, export, or otherwise transition envelopes.
Do not treat inbox payloads as admitted task evidence.
Do not expose raw secrets or unbounded envelope bodies.
acceptance_criteria: Inbox summaries are bounded and attributed to canonical read surfaces.
Secret-like values and unbounded bodies are not recorded.
Tests prove the reader performs no inbox mutation.

tasks.title: Implement readiness and evidence reference readers
tasks.goal: Implement to-data readers for carrier readiness and evidence reference summaries.
tasks.context: Narada-native sessions need reconstructable evidence visibility without storing raw provider output or transcripts.
required_work: Use operationalReadiness, reconstruct, supervisor doctor, and registration readiness as source surfaces.
Emit readiness_snapshot and evidence_ref_summary packets with evidence file refs, schema/status summaries, capability posture, and source attribution.
Ensure provider and fixture sessions reconstruct with bounded evidence refs and no raw provider output.
Add tests for fixture-backed, provider-backed, blocked, and missing-evidence states.
non_goals: Do not inspect private SQLite state as readiness truth.
Do not record raw provider output or raw transcripts.
Do not let runtime liveness become task authority.
acceptance_criteria: Readiness and evidence readers emit bounded attributed packets.
Fixture and provider sessions reconstruct from evidence refs.
Tests prove raw provider output and unbounded transcripts are not recorded.

tasks.title: Implement bounded local file excerpt reader
tasks.goal: Implement a capability-gated local text excerpt reader for admitted Site roots.
tasks.context: The carrier may need small local text excerpts, but file readability must not collapse into Narada authority or secret access.
required_work: Require an explicit site_file_excerpt_read capability reference before reading local files.
Reject traversal outside the admitted Site root, binary files, oversized excerpts, secret-like paths, and files that require a stronger canonical reader.
Emit bounded_file_excerpt packets with path attribution, byte or line bounds, redaction posture, and no-mutation flags.
Add tests for path containment, size limits, binary refusal, secret-path refusal, and excerpt redaction posture.
non_goals: Do not implement broad filesystem browsing.
Do not read credential stores, secret files, private keys, SQLite databases, or raw transcripts.
Do not treat local file content as admitted Narada truth.
acceptance_criteria: The file reader is capability-gated and Site-root-contained.
Secret-like, binary, oversized, traversal, and stronger-reader paths are refused.
Tests prove excerpts are bounded, attributed, and non-mutating.

tasks.title: Add integrated to-data adapter reconstruction proof
tasks.goal: Prove the to-data adapters compose into a bounded Narada-native data bundle.
tasks.context: After individual readers exist, the carrier needs an end-to-end mocked read bundle that can feed orchestration without carrying mutation authority.
required_work: Build an end-to-end mocked carrier session that reads task, work-next or refusal, inbox summary, readiness, evidence refs, and a bounded file excerpt into one to-data bundle.
Verify the bundle records source attribution, capability refs, freshness, and no-mutation flags for every read family.
Verify no raw secrets, unbounded transcripts, direct SQLite requirement, raw provider output, or authority-bearing mutations are recorded.
Document residuals for capability consent binding and orchestration wrapper chapters.
non_goals: Do not invoke live intelligence providers in this proof.
Do not submit task reports, inbox transitions, outbox intents, command intents, or publications.
Do not require network access for tests.
acceptance_criteria: An integrated mocked to-data bundle proof exists.
The proof covers all required read families or explicit refusal packets.
Tests prove the bundle is bounded, attributed, reconstructable, and non-mutating.
